original_image_tags: Aged, yellowish paper with visible bleed-through of typed text from the reverse side. The page contains multiple official stamps, primarily upside down relative to the main orientation. A red rectangular stamp at the bottom right reads "U.S. DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE, FEDERAL BUREAU OF INVESTIGATION, ★ APR 27 1949 P.M., W A I L E D, COMUNICATIONS SECTION". Several blue ink stamps and handwritten notations are scattered across the lower half. The top portion shows faint, reversed text bleeding through from the other side of the document.

elements.description: A scanned page containing faint, mirrored typed text and several official stamps. Visual elements include: a red rectangular stamp at the bottom reading 'COMMUNICATIONS SECTION', 'MAILED', 'APR 27 1945 P.M.', 'U.S. DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE', 'FEDERAL BUREAU OF INVESTIGATION'. A blue ink stamp reads 'APR 27 7 18 PM 1945', 'RECEIVED R.M. ROOM', 'F.B.I.', 'U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE'. Another blue ink stamp reads 'APR 27 2 35 PM 1945'. A vertical handwritten annotation reads 'E 1659'. A faint stamp in the upper right reads 'RELEASED'. Two black hole-punch circles are visible at the top edge.

judge.reason: Both sides quote specific text from the same red stamp but give contradictory dates ('APR 27 1949' vs 'APR 27 1945') and differing word order/phrases ('W A I L E D, COMUNICATIONS SECTION' vs 'MAILED', 'COMMUNICATIONS SECTION').
